heading: Aircraft supplies and services: foreign military or air attaché
label: 7626 Aircraft supplies and services: foreign military or air attaché
text: Under such conditions as he may prescribe, the Secretary of the Army may provide for the sale of fuel, oil, and other supplies for use in aircraft operated by a foreign military or air attaché accredited to the United States, and for the furnishing of mechanical service and other assistance to such aircraft. Shelter may be furnished to such aircraft, but only without charge.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)462622:259 (less last sentence).(less last sentence), .May 31, 1939, ch. 16153 Stat. 795The last sentence is substituted for the words “except for shelter for which no charge shall be made”. The words “and equipment” are omitted as covered by the word “supplies”. 22:259 (last 22 words of 2d sentence) is omitted to reflect Title IV of the National Security Act of 1947, as amended (), which authorized the Secretary of Defense to prescribe regulations governing the use and sale of certain inventories at cost, including applicable administrative expenses. (See opinion of the Assistant General Counsel (Fiscal Matters) of the Office of the Secretary of Defense, .)63 Stat. 585January 4, 1955
Amendments2018— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–232section 4626 of this title

Effective Date of 2018 AmendmentAmendment by effective , with provision for the coordination of amendments and special rule for certain redesignations, see , set out as a note preceding .Pub. L. 115–232section 800 of Pub. L. 115–232section 3001 of this titleFeb. 1, 2019
